authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

reaches.name: Butano Creek, mouth to Pescadero-Bean Hollow Road county bridge
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth (confluence with Pescadero Creek at Pescadero Marsh)
reaches.toDesc: County bridge on Pescadero-Bean Hollow Road

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 220266, "Butano Creek", Stream, CA, San Mateo Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":220266,"gaz_name":"Butano Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","state_alpha":"CA"} — multipoint geometry spanning the creek's mouth (confluence with Pescadero Creek, matching Wikipedia's GNIS-cited mouth coordinate 37°16'01"N 122°24'43"W) to its source near China Grade; first point (the mouth) is -122.4119, 37.2669, correcting the file's prior coordinate (-122.3600, 37.2480), which had no GNIS backing and sat ~5.1km away.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(b)(19) — Butano Creek
sources.quotedText: (19) Butano Creek (San Mateo Co.). Above Butano Falls. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures will barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(a) — General provisions for the special-regulations trout water list
sources.quotedText: (3) Daily bag and possession limits, unless otherwise noted, mean the total number of trout. (4) Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(22) — Butano Creek, mouth to Pescadero-Bean Hollow Road county bridge
sources.quotedText: Butano Creek (San Mateo Co.). From mouth to county bridge on Pescadero-Bean Hollow Road. Also see Section 8.00(c) Low-Flow Restrictions. Dec. 1 through Mar. 7, but only on Sat., Sun., Wed., legal holidays and opening and closing days. Only barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(a) — General provisions and footnote definitions for the anadromous/hatchery special-regulations water list
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to the take of hatchery trout, hatchery steelhead, and salmon and to fishing for these species, unless otherwise noted. ... Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: Butano State Park — CA State Parks
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Above Butano Falls: open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30, catch-and-release only; closed the rest of the year.
regulations.verbatimText: Above Butano Falls. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30.

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout — catch-and-release only, above Butano Falls.

parameters.note: Regulation text reads 'Only artificial lures will barbless hooks may be used' — apparent typographical error in the official codified text for 'with barbless hooks'.

regulations.humanSummary: Above Butano Falls: only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures will barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Open only on Saturdays, Sundays, Wednesdays, legal holidays, and the opening and closing days of the general trout season; closed all other days within the date range and closed entirely outside it.

regulations.humanSummary: Mouth to Pescadero-Bean Hollow Road county bridge: open Dec. 1 through Mar. 7, but only on Saturdays, Sundays, Wednesdays, legal holidays, and opening/closing days.
regulations.verbatimText: Dec. 1 through Mar. 7, but only on Sat., Sun., Wed., legal holidays and opening and closing days.

parameters.note: Only barbless hooks are required; unlike many special-regulation waters this reach is not restricted to artificial lures only, so bait fishing with barbless hooks is permitted.

regulations.humanSummary: Mouth to county bridge: only barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead — fish with a healed, missing adipose fin. All other (unclipped/wild) trout and steelhead must be released unharmed per the statewide §7.40(a) hatchery-fish definition.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, mouth to county bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead without a healed adipose fin clip (wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked (adipose fin-clipped) trout or steelhead may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released unharmed.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Cross-referenced to CCR §8.00(c) statewide low-flow angling closure provisions, which can close this reach to fishing when streamflow drops below regulatory thresholds.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to CCR §8.00(c) low-flow restrictions, which may close this reach when creek flows are low.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
